diagnosis_packet.problem_statement: An intraday paper sweep repeatedly selects valid stock candidates but enters none of the short ORB breakdowns. Logs classify their stops as invalid even though a representative signal has entry 300.29, stop 337.15, target 291.70, and direction short. Long stock entries and the separate option-premium path remain healthy. Diagnose where directional ownership is lost across risk sizing, sizing telemetry, and the persisted paper trade. Make the smallest complete repair without weakening stop geometry, risk limits, or duplicate-entry gates.

conclusion.claim: The observed failure is primarily caused by data drift.
conclusion.reason: Heuristic fallback only; a local investigator should challenge this ranking.

contract_invariants: Directional position ownership is normalized once and propagated unchanged through risk sizing, telemetry, and persistence. Long geometry uses stop < entry < target and loss distance entry - stop; short geometry uses target < entry < stop and loss distance stop - entry. Side-specific default stops preserve the same inequalities, and an unknown side keeps the established long-compatible behavior.
